task_completion.description: The reference management software (Zotero) is open and the collection containing the machine learning papers is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Zotero application window with a collection named 'ML References' or 'Machine Learning Papers' selected in the left sidebar, showing the relevant papers in the main pane.

task_completion.description: A generated report of the collection is visible on the screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Zotero report window or a web browser displaying a generated HTML report of the library items.

task_completion.description: The generated report contains the abstracts and metadata of the papers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Read the text within the generated report to ensure it includes metadata fields (like authors, date, publication) and the full abstract paragraphs for the listed papers.

task_completion.description: The generated report includes the specific expected machine learning papers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Verify that the report explicitly lists the titles of the expected papers: 'Attention Is All You Need', 'Deep Learning', 'Generative Adversarial Nets', and 'BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding'.

task_completion.description: The report is saved as an HTML file to the expected output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Save As' dialog box showing the directory '/home/ga/Documents/' and the filename 'reading_list.html', or a file manager/terminal window confirming the file exists at '/home/ga/Documents/reading_list.html'.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's GUI to generate the report rather than manually typing it out.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for interaction with Zotero's right-click context menus (e.g., 'Generate Report from Collection') rather than a text editor open with manually typed HTML.

integrity.description: The report appears to be genuinely generated by the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The HTML report should have the standard Zotero report layout and styling, not a barebones or manually constructed HTML structure.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no terminal windows showing python scripts or sqlite3 commands querying the Zotero database directly to build the HTML file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/reading_list.html'. Note a task specification mismatch: the target collection is named 'ML References' in the application database, despite the task description asking for 'Machine Learning Papers'. The report must include the following real papers: 'Attention Is All You Need', 'Deep Learning', 'Generative Adversarial Nets', and 'BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding'.
